title: Conjecture 1.1 (r-Quasiplanar Graph Conjecture)
statement_text: The number of edges of every $r$-quasiplanar graph of $n$ vertices is $O_r(n)$.
context_text: Described as a longstanding unsolved problem in the theory of topological graphs. It has been verified for $r \leq 4$. The paper's Theorem 1.7 provides an improved upper bound for $r = 2s$, $s \geq 3$, giving at most $n\bigl(\frac{c\log n}{s}\bigr)^{2s-4}$ edges, which substantially beats the previously best known bounds of $n(\log n)^{O(\log r)}$ and $O(n(\log n)^{4r-16})$.
attributed_to: Combinatorial geometry community; see Brass, Moser, and Pach [BrMP05]
paper_contribution: Proves that every $r$-quasiplanar graph with $n \geq r$ vertices, for $r = 2s$ and $s \geq 3$, has at most $n\bigl(\frac{c\log n}{s}\bigr)^{2s-4}$ edges, where $c > 0$ is an absolute constant, improving all previously known upper bounds.
notes: Header carries no parenthetical attribution, but the surrounding text explicitly labels this a longstanding open problem with reference [BrMP05]; treated as 'studies' accordingly.
paper_title: Quasiplanar Graphs, String Graphs, and the Erdos-Gallai Problem

title: Problem 1.2
statement_text: Fix an integer $r \geq 4$. Is it true that every $K_r$-free segment graph on $n$ vertices has an induced subgraph on $\Omega_r(n)$ vertices which is $K_{r-1}$-free?
context_text: Motivated by the approach of coloring $K_r$-free string graphs to prove Conjecture 1.1: if each color class could be made $K_4$-free, Ackerman's result would bound the edges per class by $O(n)$. Although Krawczyk–Walczak showed $K_r$-free string graphs can require $\Omega_r(\log\log n)$ colors with $K_{r-1}$-free classes, the question of finding a linearly large $K_{r-1}$-free induced subgraph in a $K_r$-free segment graph remains open. The paper's Theorems 1.5 and 1.6 address analogous questions for string graphs (with a $\log^2 n$ loss), but the segment-graph case with a linear guarantee is unresolved.
paper_title: Quasiplanar Graphs, String Graphs, and the Erdos-Gallai Problem
